description_long: This full-height turnstile features a cylindrical frame with a series of evenly spaced vertical metal bars forming a secure enclosure from top to bottom. The structure is capped with a flat, polygonal metal cover and a matching base, both of which are solid and robust. At the center, a vertical axis supports multiple horizontal rotating arms, which are arranged in a radial pattern and serve as the primary barrier mechanism. One side of the turnstile includes a rectangular access control panel, likely for card or biometric entry, integrated into the frame. The overall design is industrial and utilitarian, suitable for high-security urban environments such as subway stations, stadiums, or building entrances. The materials appear to be primarily metal, with a smooth, hard finish and a monochrome color scheme.
description: A tall, cylindrical full-height turnstile with vertical metal bars, a flat metal top and base, and a central rotating arm mechanism. It features an integrated access control panel and a robust, secure design.
description_view_0: Front view showing the access control panel and the central rotating arms clearly visible through the bars.
description_view_1: Side view with the access control panel visible on the left and the cylindrical frame's vertical bars dominating the profile.
description_view_2: Rear view highlighting the central axis and rotating arms, with the access control panel not visible.
description_view_3: Opposite side view with the access control panel on the right, showing the cylindrical enclosure and vertical bars.
